task-1775784087543-d9qtak.description: Analyze broker/adapters/ruflo.py and broker/adapters/supermemory.py stubs. Compare them against the broker engine interface. Determine which adapter is easier to replace first with a real integration, considering that Ruflo MCP memory tools are already available in this session. List the specific MCP tools that map to each adapter method (upsert, retrieve).

result.finding: Ruflo adapter is easier to replace first because Ruflo MCP memory tools are already live in this session

mapping.upsert: mcp__ruflo__memory_store with key=record.id, namespace=record.scope, value=record.to_dict(), tags from subject, upsert=true
mapping.retrieve: mcp__ruflo__memory_search with query=scope, namespace=scope, plus mcp__ruflo__memory_retrieve for exact key lookup

result.supermemory_status: Requires API key and hosted/self-hosted backend — not available in this session yet
result.recommendation: Replace Ruflo stub first, keep Supermemory stub until API key is configured

task-1775784087565-rbckdt.description: Draft a concrete implementation plan for replacing the Ruflo adapter stub (broker/adapters/ruflo.py) with calls to Ruflo MCP memory tools: memory_store for upsert and memory_search for retrieve. Include the field mapping from MemoryRecord to Ruflo memory_store parameters and from Ruflo search results back to the broker retrieval format. Identify any gaps or missing fields.

result.plan: Replace RufloBackend stub methods with real MCP calls via subprocess or HTTP

field_mapping_retrieve.results[].value: parsed back into broker record dicts

gaps: memory_store has no native user_id/workspace_id fields — must embed in value or use namespace convention like scope:user:workspace
memory_search similarity threshold may need tuning — test returned 0.31 similarity
384-dim embeddings vs config expecting 768 — HNSW config mismatch is cosmetic only
result.blocker: RufloBackend in broker runs as Python library — cannot directly call MCP tools. Integration path: either (a) broker calls Ruflo via HTTP when broker runs as service, or (b) broker uses the same sqlite DB that Ruflo MCP uses, or (c) broker spawns npx ruflo commands. Option (b) is simplest for local-first MVP.
